title: AI Trade Tool - MT5 Integrated
author: Due-Mousse-5794
selftext:
I've been developing a Python-based trading system integrated with MetaTrader 5 that executes trades automatically.

Currently, I'm focusing on improving risk management, drawdown control, and testing robustness across different market conditions.

One challenge I'm working on is ensuring that performance remains stable with limited sample sizes and avoiding overfitting during optimization.

For those with experience in systematic trading or quantitative research:

What methods do you typically use to validate strategy robustness before moving from demo to live trading?

For example:

* Minimum number of trades
* Walk-forward testing
* Monte Carlo simulations
* Risk-adjusted performance metrics

Would appreciate any insights or best practices.

comments.author: Actual_Resort1892
comments.body: Is it a backtesting tool? Or execution system

replies.author: Due-Mousse-5794
replies.body: This system is primarily designed as an execution system for automated live trading via MetaTrader 5 (MT5), using dedicated engines for control, execution, risk, and market structure. It also includes a backtesting component that allows the same trading logic to be simulated on historical CSV data without connecting to MT5 :)
